question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the /network/status endpoint with concurrent requests, exhausting database connection slots via repeated calls to addressBookEntryRepo.Entries(ctx) and blockRepo.RetrieveLatest(ctx), causing the service to become unavailable to all other clients and effectively partitioning them from network state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition by sending a high volume of /network/status requests that each invoke RetrieveGenesis(ctx) and RetrieveLatest(ctx) simultaneously, exhausting the DB connection pool and causing all legitimate clients to receive ErrDatabaseError, making the network appear unreachable?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending crafted /network/status requests with a context that times out immediately, causing addressBookEntryRepo.Entries(ctx) to return an error and the service to return nil peers, making downstream Rosetta clients believe the network has zero peers and is partitioned?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential dependency of RetrieveGenesis(ctx) followed by RetrieveLatest(ctx) in NetworkStatus() by flooding the endpoint to cause one call to succeed and the other to fail, returning a partial response that causes Rosetta clients to compute an incorrect chain tip and diverge from the real network state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a sustained network partition by sending requests that force addressBookEntryRepo.Entries(ctx) to return an empty peer list (when the DB query returns zero rows for both addressBook101 and addressBook102), causing all Rosetta clients to see no peers and stop routing transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of rate limiting on /network/status to cause a resource exhaustion attack where each request triggers three separate DB queries (RetrieveGenesis, RetrieveLatest, Entries), multiplying DB load by 3x per request and causing a network partition by making the mirror node DB unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that trigger the ErrNodeIsStarting error path, causing Rosetta clients to believe the network node is permanently starting and never attempt to reconnect, effectively partitioning them from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that NetworkStatus() returns CurrentBlockTimestamp from currentBlock.GetTimestampMillis() without any staleness check, causing clients to believe the network is live when the mirror node is actually lagging far behind the real chain?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause RetrieveLatest(ctx) to return a block with a timestamp far in the past, making Rosetta clients believe the network has stalled and refuse to submit transactions, effectively partitioning them from the live network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the absence of any check that GenesisBlockIdentifier and CurrentBlockIdentifier are consistent (e.g., current index >= genesis index), causing clients to receive logically impossible block ranges and diverge from the real network state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by repeatedly calling /network/options to enumerate all errors.Errors entries (including retriable ones like ErrNodeIsStarting, ErrDatabaseError) and use that information to craft requests that force the service into a retriable error loop, causing clients to endlessly retry and never reach the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NetworkOptions() exposes the full errors.Errors slice (including ErrNodeAccountIdsEmpty with Retriable:true) to fingerprint the service state and craft targeted requests that trigger those retriable errors at scale, causing a network partition through sustained error amplification?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by flooding /network/options with requests that force repeated calls to types.GetTransactionResults() and GetStringValuesFromInt32StringMap(), exhausting CPU and causing the service to stop responding to /network/status requests that clients need to discover peers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HistoricalBalanceLookup: true flag exposed in NetworkOptions() to craft a large number of historical balance queries that exhaust DB resources, indirectly causing NetworkStatus() to fail and partitioning clients from current network state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkList()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by flooding /network/list with requests, since NetworkList() has no rate limiting and returns n.network directly, exhausting HTTP handler goroutines and preventing legitimate clients from reaching /network/status to discover peers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkList()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NetworkList() returns a single hardcoded n.network identifier with no validation, causing clients that receive a manipulated or stale network identifier to connect to the wrong network and become partitioned from the real network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NewNetworkAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that operationTypes is built once at construction time via GetStringValuesFromInt32StringMap(types.GetTransactionTypes()) and never refreshed, causing clients to receive stale operation type lists that diverge from the live network's supported operations?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests with a crafted context deadline that is just long enough for RetrieveGenesis(ctx) to succeed but causes RetrieveLatest(ctx) to time out, resulting in a partial NetworkStatusResponse that causes clients to compute an incorrect sync status and stop routing to the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that peers.ToRosetta() in NetworkStatus() converts AddressBookEntries to Rosetta Peers without any deduplication or validation of endpoint strings, causing clients to receive duplicate or malformed peer entries that prevent them from connecting to real network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the Peers list returned by NetworkStatus() is sourced from the mirror node DB (addressBookEntryRepo.Entries) rather than live node discovery, causing clients to receive a stale peer list that no longer reflects the current network topology and partitioning them from active nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that trigger ErrDatabaseError (retriable: true) in addressBookEntryRepo.Entries(ctx), causing Rosetta clients configured to retry on retriable errors to enter an infinite retry loop and never successfully discover peers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that NetworkStatus() does not validate that the returned peers list is non-empty before returning a NetworkStatusResponse, causing clients to receive a valid 200 response with zero peers and believe the network has no reachable nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending a burst of /network/status requests that each hold a DB transaction open via GetDbWithContext(ctx), exhausting the DB connection pool and causing all subsequent requests to block indefinitely, partitioning all clients from network state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that IsOnline() only checks if blockRepo != nil (a static initialization check) and never verifies live connectivity to the underlying data source, causing the service to report online status and return stale data even when the mirror node is actually disconnected from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the gap between IsOnline() returning true (blockRepo != nil) and the actual DB being unreachable, causing NetworkStatus() to attempt DB queries that all fail with ErrDatabaseError, making the network appear partitioned to all clients?
